skills.name: workflow_planner
skills.description: Convert user requests into automation workflows.
skills.when_to_use: Use when the user describes an automation or integration.
rules: Supported triggers: form.submitted, github.issue_created, stripe.payment_succeeded, email.received, webhook.received.
The trigger must be an object with a 'type' field (e.g., { "type": "form.submitted" }).
CRITICAL: When the user request contains words like 'if', 'only if', 'when', 'conditionally', you MUST include a 'logic' array with a condition step having 'type': 'condition.if'.
Supported logic types: condition.if, filter.data, delay.wait, transform.data.
Actions must follow the trigger. Each action must be an object with an 'action' field (e.g., { "action": "slack.send_message" }).
Supported actions: slack.send_message, email.send, sms.send, webhook.send, database.create, database.update, database.read, notion.create_page, google_sheets.add_row, calendar.create_event.
Always include a 'description' field explaining the workflow.
Always include a 'diagram' field with a Mermaid graph TD diagram as a single-line escaped string (e.g., "diagram": "graph TD\nA[Trigger] --> B[Action]").
Return ONLY valid JSON without any other text.

logic: array of logic step objects with 'type' field
actions: array of action objects with 'action' field
output_format.diagram: mermaid diagram as single-line escaped string

skills.name: diagram_generator
skills.description: Create a Mermaid diagram to visualize the workflow.
skills.when_to_use: Use after a workflow is generated.
